q: A macromolecule is a very large molecule that contains ___ of atoms or more.
A ___ is a type of long-chain macromolecule that is linked together by many small repeat units known as monomers.
If a polymer consists of only one kind of monomers then it is called a ___.
A polymer which consists of more than one kind of monomers is called a ___.
The process of joining together a large number of monomers to form a polymer is called ___.
___ polymerisation occurs when unsaturated monomers join together without losing any molecules or atoms.
___ polymerisation occurs when the monomers combine to form a polymer, with the removal of a small molecule such as water.
The simplest addition polymer is ___.
The formula of a polymer can be written using its ___ (monomer).
Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is produced by the addition polymerisation of ___ monomers.
Addition polymers are sometimes referred to as ___.
At low temperatures, addition polymers lose plasticity and elasticity and become ___.
Teflon is heat-resistant and has ___ properties.
Polystyrene is also known as ___.
___ is an example of polyesters made from the reaction of an acid monomer and an alcohol monomer.
Nylon is an example of ___, which are first synthetic fibres.
The covalent bond formed between two amino acids is called a ___ bond.
Plastics are non-biodegradable as they cannot be broken down by ___ in the soil.
Upon incineration, ___ produces corrosive hydrogen chloride gas during combustion.
Turtles often mistake plastic bags for ___ and choke on them.
___ symbols consist of three arrows around one of seven numbers.
___ plastics are made from monomers derived directly from plant materials.
PLA is made from fermented plant ___.
___ polymer chains incorporate carbonyl groups ($C=O$) at intervals down their length.
When an ant bites, it injects the acidic liquid ___ acid into the skin.
Silk comes from the ___ of the silkworm.
